name: Pu'u Ma'eli'eli Trail
tagline: Kailua's lesser-known pillbox hike with the same views and a fraction of the crowds.
parking: Free street parking on Kapa'a Quarry Road near the trailhead
bestTime: Sunrise for golden light over Kailua Bay and the Mokulua Islands
talkStory: About 2.5 miles round trip with 600 ft of elevation gain — short but steep in spots. You'll pass three WWII bunkers along the ridge with panoramic views of Kailua, Kaneohe Bay, and the Ko'olau Range. Way less crowded than the Lanikai Pillbox and honestly the views are just as good. The trail is well-maintained with some rocky sections. There's a bonus loop option that extends the hike along the ridge. Locals come here for quick after-work sunset sessions.
